content.text: Commander and provocateur. In combat: buff allies, debuff enemies, shape every engagement. Abilities are your primary weapon — augments multiply them. Between combat: the crew's face for every interaction.
Read the Room (Passive): At the start of combat, identify which enemy is the leader, which has the lowest morale, and which has the lowest Grit. You always know who to target.

Rally (Active, 1 AP): [Commander] Choose an ally within Medium range. Remove one of: Stunned, Prone (stand them up for free), Dazed, or 1 stack of any condition. Your voice cuts through the chaos. This is the Negotiator's core support verb — cheap, flexible, always useful.

Taunt (Active, 1 AP): [Provocateur] Edge check vs target's Grit within Medium range. On success: target must attack you or move toward you on their next turn if able. On failure: no effect. This is the Negotiator's core disruption verb — force enemies to make bad decisions.
T2 — Trained Techniques (300 cr each, 1 downtime)

rows: An ally within Medium range gets +1 accuracy AND +1 damage per die on their next attack this round. You call the shot. Expires at end of round if unused.
An ally within Medium range may immediately move 1 range band as a free action (doesn't cost their AP, doesn't provoke opportunity attacks). You direct the chess pieces.
An ally within Medium range gains +2 Evasion against the next attack targeting them. You warned them in time. Expires at end of round.
Edge check vs target's Grit within Medium range. On success: -2 accuracy on their next attack. You got in their head.
Edge check vs target's Edge within Medium range. On success: target cannot use special abilities or augment activations on their next turn. Their timing is off, their focus is broken.
Edge check (DC 7) vs a target within Medium range. On success: target loses 1 AP on their next turn. You waste their time with misdirection.
When you successfully use any Provocateur ability on an enemy, you also learn their current HP range (full/above half/below half/critical) and all active conditions. Free intel with every debuff.

content.text: T3 — Advanced Techniques (600 cr each, 2 downtime)

rows: ALL allies within Medium range get +1 accuracy until end of your next turn. The whole squad sharpens up. Once per encounter.
Designate an ally. Until start of your next turn, that ally gains +1 Armor (you're calling out incoming threats, they're bracing on your timing).
Designate an ally within Close range. The next attack that targets them targets you instead. You step in. Once per encounter.
Designate a target. All allies that hit this target before your next turn deal +1 damage per die. The squad concentrates.
When an enemy hits an ally within Medium range, reduce the damage by 1d6. Your insult throws off their strike. Uses your Reaction. Once per round.
Edge check (DC 7). Hordes break at 25% casualties instead of 50%. Against a Standard enemy below 25% HP: they surrender entirely on success. Doesn't work on bosses, elites, or mindless enemies.

Choose two enemies within Medium range. Edge check (DC 8). On success: those enemies cannot support each other (no flanking bonuses, no horde bonuses, no coordinated abilities) for 2 rounds. You break their formation.
Your Provocateur abilities last 1 additional round against Boss-tier enemies. Bosses have the biggest egos — and the most to lose when you crack them.

content.text: T4 — Master Techniques (1,000 cr each, 3 downtime)

rows: At the start of each round (free, before anyone acts): choose one — reposition an ally 1 range band, grant an ally +2 to their next check, or give an ally 1 temporary AP this turn. Your tactical mind works before the fighting starts.
Allies within Close range: +1 to all checks. Enemies within Close range: -1 to all checks. Always on. Your presence warps the fight.

Edge check vs Grit on a Standard-or-lower enemy within Medium range. On success: they fight for you for 2 rounds with their full stat block. Once per encounter. Doesn't work on bosses, elites, or mindless enemies.
Edge check vs Grit on a target within Medium range. On success: target cannot use special abilities, augment activations, OR reactions for 2 rounds. Total shutdown. Once per encounter.
Edge check vs Edge on a target within Medium range. On success: you choose the target's next attack — who they hit and with what weapon. They attack their own ally. Once per encounter.
